entry.title: Re: Unused Budget per Planner from Previous Compensation...
entry.content:
Hi @David,

Thank you for your feedback. I took into consideration your recommendation about using the cascade budget approach. However, after further clarification with the client, we confirmed that the budget is assigned based on the Head of each Department/Division (SVP level), and the remaining budget should stay with that planner regardless of any future population changes.

I also noticed that, as you mentioned, the cascade budget approach requires significant manual effort. For example, some planners may not have more than two levels below them in the hierarchy, meaning the budget does not cascade automatically to the entire intended population.

Based on this, I was thinking about an alternative approach: calculate the remaining budget after the January cycle (Assigned Budget - Total Increase), export this information, and use it as the starting budget for the July cycle.

Have you seen this approach used in other implementations, or would you recommend a different way to handle this scenario?

entry.title: Re: Joule Custom MDF Redirection Request
entry.content:
Hi Maria,
Currently, there is one available Joule use case related to custom MDF objects which is "Approve MDF Workflow Requests". It supports workflow approvals for both custom and pre-delivered MDF objects. For more information, please refer to the Platform Use Cases Help Guide.
If you would like to see additional Joule use cases for custom MDF objects in future releases, we recommend submitting an enhancement request through the Customer Influence Portal under SAP SuccessFactors Platform, Foundation and Technology -> Metadata Framework (MDF) category. Enhancement requests are reviewed by the Product Management team and may be considered for future development and product releases based on customer demand and product strategy. For detailed instructions on how to submit an enhancement request, please refer to KBA 2090228.
In addition, you may want to explore Joule Studio, which provides capabilities for creating custom Joule experiences. We recommend evaluating whether it meets the requirements of your specific use case. To learn more, here you can find a few resources:
The Complete Joule Studio Resource Hub: Everything You Need to Get Started
Joule Studio, classic edition
We hope this information is helpful.

entry.title: Re: Predictive Accounting for incoming sales orders in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We have reviewed the official documentation for Predictive Accounting in SAP S/4HANA On‑Premise as well as SAP Notes 3223551 and 3653948.

Based on this information, our understanding is the following:

In SAP S/4HANA On‑Premise, predictive journal entries are only created correctly at the time of the goods issue.

The customer invoice only reduces an existing predictive journal entry and does not create a predictive entry if no goods issue has taken place.

There is no functionality in On‑Premise (including Fiori applications) to simulate or recalculate predictive journal entries by changing material prices.

Changing the material price in the master data does not update existing predictive entries; the prediction is only recalculated when the corresponding sales order item is changed.

Could you please confirm that in SAP S/4HANA On‑Premise 2025 the process flow Sales Order → Goods Issue → Customer Invoice is mandatory for the creation and reduction of predictive journal entries, and that the capability to generate predictive entries directly from the sales order without a goods issue (Order-Based Predictive Accounting) is available only in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and no longer part of SAP S/4HANA On‑Premise?  Best regards, Gustavo

entry.title: Re: Check number is not updating in Assignment filed of G...
entry.content:
Hello @Inthiyaz1 

Thank you for your question

 

In Public Cloud, the check number and journal entry assignment field are separate fields. There is no standard setting that automatically copies the check number into the Assignment field to the bank G/L line item for every payment and bank statement process.

For bank reconciliation, copying the value is usually unnecessary. Bank statement interpretation and matching function are provided so that you can use the Check Number directly. For example, supported interpretation logic can search open items by using an assignment number derived from the bank statement’s Customer Reference Number, Check Number, or memo line.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/19f2847b2d9548d29fa89996e1233126.html?locale=en-US

Here are steps you should consider,

Ensure you confirm whether the bank statement is available in the imported statement

In Reprocess Bank Statement Items, you can select the relevant house bank, statement, and item.

Ensure you check whether Check Number is populated in the bank statement details.

If the value is present, you can configure the reconciliation logic to use it directly rather than needing it in the G/L Assignment field. In the relevant bank statement processing rule or matching instruction, you can use Check Number (Bank Statement) as a source field for matching against the appropriate open item field, bank statements fields including Assignment Reference Check Number, Customer Reference Number, and End to End identification are added as available matching sources.

Ensure you verify the imported statement format mapping. If the bank provides the check number but the field is empty, you can review the mapping so that the source value is assigned to the bank statement Check Number field.  

You should only consider making use of Manage Substitution/Validation Rules to populate the standard Assignment field when the required source field and target are officially available in the relevant journal entry substitution context. Substitution rules for defined FI posting contexts, but this does not mean that the bank statement check number is available as a source for substituting Assignment during the generated bank posting.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c56f622a2edf491b9f1b596b55587009/5c8c2825535f4e3aa9b7f716a0085221.html?locale=en-US

Therefore, you do not assume that a substitution can perform this copy without first confirming the available fields.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Engineering Change Managem...
entry.content:
Good day @ParakhMahendiratta 

 

Thank you for your question

 

Question 1: Is this the standard behavior in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

Answer: Yes, in the delivered Change Record Discrete Industry process, the first is the approval of change request. The engineer provides the reason, affected objects, dates, attachments, and other information required for that decision. The change number is then generated for the implementation phase, where the BOM is changed with reference to that number

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/3ba32a4dfb1e4a4e8d3bc288a1640ef0.html?locale=en-US

Question 2: If yes, does the approver only approve the engineering request rather than the actual BOM modifications?

Answer: At the initial approval stage, yes. The approver is primarily deciding whether the requested engineering work should proceed. The actual BOM component changes have not yet been implemented under the generated change number.

Question 3: Is there any standard process that allows the approver to review the proposed BOM changes before approval?

Answer: The better option is to separate request approval from implementation acceptance. After approval, you can start implementation, change the BOM with the generated change number and future valid from date, then route the change record through an acceptance or completion step. Change records workflow can control process steps from initiation through acceptance and execution, responsible users and permitted actions.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/f397ae39479844f78b100d79aea35a51.html?locale=en-US

The approver can open the assigned BOM from Manage Change Records and review its component information. However, the system does not take record of a configurable option that moves standard change number generation before the initial request approval while retaining the delivered process semantics. The configuration activity Change Number handling at Change Record Item controls handling by item type, you cannot redesign the approval sequence.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/d1c63dbe742b45ad98efa0e726da74a5.html?locale=en-US

Here is a practical controlled process:

Ensure you create and approve the change request, then start implementation and generate the change number.

In Maintain Bills Material, Version 2, ensure you assign the change record or change the number and enter the future valid from date.

You can implement additions, deletions, quality changes, and replacements and send the record to one approver for acceptance.

Ensure you close or release the implementation only after approval. BOM supports BOM maintenance and assignment of change records.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/68ace4fb90454696863aa9041d27c646.html?locale=en-US

 

Question 4: Is it possible to configure the workflow so that:

  • the Change Number is available before approval,
  • the engineer prepares the BOM changes,
  • and the approver reviews the modified BOM before approving and releasing it?

Answer: You can use Maintenance of Bills of Material Items. It is used for editing or deleting items across multiple BOMs, simulation before execution, and synchronous or background processing.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/702c12d24ac34a37b7a6bacfd6bd6176.html?locale=en-US

Question 5: If this is not supported in the standard Public Cloud process, what is the SAP-recommended approach for customers who require approval of the actual engineering changes rather than only the request?

Answer: The better sequence is approval, change number generation, simulation, controlled mass execution, result review in Monitor mass Maintenance of Bills Material, and final acceptance.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Power BI Data Refresh through SAP BW Process Chain
entry.content:
Introduction

In many SAP BW projects, data loading is orchestrated by SAP BW process chains, while business users consume the data through Microsoft Power BI dashboards. One common requirement is to automatically refresh the Power BI dataset immediately after the BW process chain finishes successfully.

While the Power BI Service exposes REST APIs for triggering dataset refreshes, these APIs are not available in Power BI Report Server. As a result, a different approach is required.

In this blog post, I will demonstrate a simple solution for triggering a Microsoft Power BI Report Server data refresh directly from an SAP BW process chain.

 

 

Solution Overview

The implementation consists of the following steps:

  1. Create a data flow in SAP BW.
  2. Create a SQL Server stored procedure that triggers the Power BI dataset refresh.
  3. Develop an ABAP report that executes the stored procedure.
  4. Add the ABAP report to an SAP BW process chain.

 

 

Sample Scenario

For demonstration purposes, I created a simple end-to-end data flow. This is the flow I've developed:

  1. Excel [Local]
  2. DSO [BW]
  3. Query [BW]
  4. Dashboard [Power BI]

 

 

Creating Stored Procedure in Power BI Report Server

The Power BI dashboard used in this example is named "Dashboard to Test PBI Data Refresh".

A scheduled refresh has already been configured for this dashboard. Instead of waiting for the scheduled execution time, we can trigger the refresh by calling the AddEvent procedure in the Report Server database.

The following stored procedure encapsulates this logic.

USE [ReportServer]
GO

SET ANSI_NULLS ON
GO

SET QUOTED_IDENTIFIER ON
GO

CREATE OR ALTER PROCEDURE [dbo].[MyRefreshData]
AS
BEGIN
SET NOCOUNT ON;

DECLARE @DASHBOARD_NAME  NVARCHAR(100),
@DASHBOARD_ID    VARCHAR(100),
@SUBSCRIPTION_ID VARCHAR(100),
@RETURN_VALUE    INT

SET @DASHBOARD_NAME = 'Dashboard to Test PBI Data Refresh'

SET @DASHBOARD_ID = (
 SELECT TOP 1 [ItemID]
 FROM [ReportServer].[dbo].[Catalog]
 WHERE [Name] = @DASHBOARD_NAME
)

SET @Subscription_ID = (
 SELECT TOP 1 SubscriptionID
 FROM [ReportServer].[dbo].[ReportSchedule]
 WHERE [ReportID] = @DASHBOARD_ID
)

WAITFOR DELAY '0:0:3'

EXEC @RETURN_VALUE = [ReportServer].[dbo].[AddEvent]
 @EventType = N'DataModelRefresh',
 @EventData = @Subscription_ID

END

Note: Replace "Dashboard to Test PBI Data Refresh" with the name of your own Power BI report.

 

 

Executing Stored Procedure from SAP BW

Next, we need an ABAP report that executes the stored procedure.

Before running the report, create a secondary database connection to the SQL Server database using transaction DBCO.

The following ABAP report opens the database connection, executes the stored procedure, and then closes the connection.

REPORT ZPBI_DATA_REFRESH.

DATA: LV_DBCO  TYPE DBCON_NAME.

LV_DBCO = 'SQL206'.

*Open database connection
EXEC SQL.
  CONNECT to :LV_DBCO
ENDEXEC.

IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
  MESSAGE 'Error in opening the connection' TYPE 'E'.
ENDIF.


*Execute the proceudre
EXEC SQL.
  execute reportserver.dbo.myrefreshdata
ENDEXEC.

IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
  MESSAGE 'Error in executing the procedure' TYPE 'E'.
ENDIF.


*Close database connection
EXEC SQL.
  DISCONNECT :LV_DBCO
ENDEXEC.

IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
  MESSAGE 'Error in closing the connection' TYPE 'E'.
ENDIF.

 

 

Adding ABAP Program to the Process Chain

The final step is to execute the ABAP report automatically from the SAP BW process chain.

Create an ABAP Program process variant, assign program ZPBI_DATA_REFRESH, and place it after the successful completion of your BW data loading processes.

With this configuration, every successful execution of the process chain automatically triggers the Power BI Report Server dataset refresh, ensuring that business users always see the latest data without any manual intervention.

 

 

Conclusion

This approach provides a straightforward integration between SAP BW and Power BI Report Server by leveraging a SQL Server stored procedure and a simple ABAP report. If you are using Power BI Report Server together with SAP BW, this technique offers a reliable way to synchronize your ETL process with Power BI data refreshes.

entry.title: Re: GR Label Printing Based on “Number of Labels” Field
entry.content:
Hello @kkumara 

 

Thank you for your question

 

Yes, Public Cloud supports your requirement. For the goods receipt label printing, your user can manually enter the required value in the Number of Labels field. When a manual value is entered, the system uses that value instead of recalculating the label count from the goods receipt quantity.

See the following reference for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/53b92207de734253ab34ff5200fd372b.html?locale=en-US

For your example

  1. Goods receipt quantity: 100
  2. Number of labels: 2
  3. Expected output: 2 labels

Here are steps to consider:

In Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document, ensure you enter the purchase order and select the relevant item.

You can enter the received quantity, in the printing section and change Printing from No print to the required option, such as individual slip.

Ensure you enter 2 in the number of labels and post the goods receipt.

Before testing, verify the product master data. The Label Type and Label Form fields should be maintained in the plant specific product data before goods receipt can be printed.

Here are items you should check: Purchase order and item, material, goods receipt quantity 100, printing section, and number of labels.

After you have posted, open Display Material Document List and select the material.

You can choose More—Extra—Output and confirm that the generated Goods Receipt label output contains two labels and capture the output status and PDF preview. Output review function inventory Management output control is made available. You cannot control the count through Output Parameter Determination or printer copy settings to determine the output type, channel, printer, and form template, while the business label quantity is supplied during goods receipt entry.

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/b7f02d1514ff4a44b144ea214556fdaf.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/b7f02d1514ff4a44b144ea214556fdaf.html?locale=en-US

If it still prints 100 Labels after entering 2, ensure you confirm that the generated output is Goods Receipt label, rather than a custom form that repeats records according to the received quantity. The custom layout or its data binding would then need correction.

  

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Is there any way to get the value of material master...
entry.content:
Good day @Iris59 

Thank you for your question

Your observation is correct. Note that in Public Cloud, the released CDS view I ProductUnitsOfMeasure exposes standard unit of measure information such as alternative unit, numerator, denominator, weight, volume, and EAN data. It does not expose the batch specific unit indicators corresponding to the MARM fields you sent such as:

  • LUn for leading Batch specific Unit of Measure
  • VUn for Valuation Based on Batch specific unit of Measure

The standard CDS field lists confirms that these indicators are not part of the released interface.

Having direct access to table MARM is also permitted in custom development in the cloud environment. Custom CDS views and ABAP Cloud developments can consume only objects released for the relevant extensibility use case, normally with C1 release status.

See the following references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/993c8fc86d3c4d8687688d74577fb6a5.html?locale=en-US

The indicators themselves are supported functionally. Lun is maintained for the leading batch specific unit and that Vun identifies a unit relevant for batch specific valuation. The limitation is specifically the absence of these fields from a released interface.

See the following references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/75ffb753128eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

Here are objects you should verify:

In View Browser, search for I ProductUnitsOfMeasure and review the Element and Annotation tabs.

You can search additionally for terms such as Batch specific Unit, Leading Unit, and Valuation Unit.

Ensure you confirm whether any new release specific CDS view exposes the required indicators.

See the following references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/0bde695751505c08e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

If no released object contains these fields, then there is currently no supported standard method to read them in custom code. A custom CDS view cannot add fields that are absent from its released source.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Commodity Code values not displayed in CDS view I_PUR...
entry.content:
Good day @Michelle1975 

 

Thank you for your question

 

Your observation is correct. The released CDS view I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01 does not provide the purchase order item’s commodity code as an exposes field. The fact that the commodity code is visible in the purchase order does not mean that it is automatically available in every released purchase order CDS interface. I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01 is the released purchase order item view, but its public field scope is fixed.

See the following references for more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/6d471dfc743048c3891601fa8ab0dc1e.html?locale=en-US

In the standard purchasing process, the commodity code is displayed at purchase order item level under Material Data. For a product-based item, It is derived from the product classification and cannot be changed directly in the purchase order. For an item without a product number, you can enter it manually.

See the following references for more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6dd96f68c48e4c139cda7b1bac5599b1/0b5bcd3c64c24040aab6f6bfb7424daa.html?locale=en-US

Here are areas you should consider:

When your requirement is to retrieve the commodity code assigned to the product, you can use a released product classification CDS view rather than expecting it from the purchase order item view. SAP provides Product Commodity Code Data Extraction, technical name I_ProdCommodityCodeDEX for retrieving products and their assigned commodity codes. The view is C1 released.

See the following references for more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/ccf21a569e484f7ab65a7ecaea508081.html?locale=en-US

A custom query can combine:

  • I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01 for purchase order, item, product, plant, and document information
  • I_ProdCommodityCodeDEX for the commodity code assigned to the product.

You should design the join and date logic carefully because commodity code classification can be time dependent and may depend on the relevant numbering scheme or country. You should not assume that product’s current commodity code always represents the exact value applicable when an older purchase order was created.

In View Browser, search for I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01 and ensure you confirm that the commodity code is not listed under commodity code fields.

Search for I_ProdCommodityCodeDEX and you can check its release status, supported capabilities, product key, validity fields, numbering scheme, and commodity code fields.

You can create custom CDS view only if both sources support the intended extensibility scenario.

 

 Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.content:
SAP inside track Brazil

O SAP Inside Track é um evento comunitário focado em soluções SAP, organizado por membros da comunidade SAP. É aberto a todos, incluindo clientes, consultores e freelancers. Este evento, que começou em 2009, é uma oportunidade para profissionais do setor se reunirem, discutirem tendências e inovações, e compartilharem conhecimento sobre SAP. O SAP Inside Track acontece em várias cidades ao redor do mundo, promovendo um ambiente colaborativo e de aprendizado.

entry.title: Re: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 2 — Roll & Move
entry.content:
1. game-service.js

const cds = require('@sap/cds')

module.exports = class GameService extends cds.ApplicationService {
  async init() {

    const { Players, BoardSquares } = this.entities

    // ── startTurn ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Waiting]  →  CAP validates status before this runs
    // @TO: #Playing      →  CAP updates status after this returns
    // No business logic needed — CAP's @flow.status handles the transition.
    this.on('startTurn', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // Intentionally empty: the @from/@to annotations do all the work.
    })

    // ── rollDice ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Playing]  →  CAP validates status before this runs
    // @TO: #Moving       →  CAP updates status after this returns
    this.on('rollDice', 'Players', async (req) => {
      const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
      if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

      const roll = Math.ceil(Math.random() * 6)
      let newPosition = player.position + roll

      // Can't overshoot square 100 — must land exactly
      if (newPosition > 100) {
        await UPDATE(Players).set({ lastRoll: roll }).where({ ID: player.ID })
        const needed = 100 - player.position
        return req.reject(409, `Need exactly ${needed} to finish. Rolled ${roll}. No move.`)
      }

      // Resolve snake or ladder on the new square
      const square = await SELECT.one.from(BoardSquares).where({ square: newPosition })
      let finalPosition = newPosition
      let event = null

      if (square?.ladderTo) {
        finalPosition = square.ladderTo
        event = { type: 'ladder', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
      } else if (square?.snakeTo) {
        finalPosition = square.snakeTo
        event = square.isDoubleHead
          ? { type: 'doubleSnake', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
          : { type: 'snake', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
      }

      // Persist position and last roll
      await UPDATE(Players)
        .set({ position: finalPosition, lastRoll: roll })
        .where({ ID: player.ID })

      // Emit domain event so subscribers (Week 3) can react
      if (event) await this.emit('BoardEvent', { playerID: player.ID, ...event })

      // Use req.reply() — not return — to pin the response value before CAP's
      // @flow.status after-handler runs. A plain `return` gets cleared by the
      // flow machinery, producing HTTP 204 instead of the expected 200 + body.
      req.reply({ roll, position: finalPosition, event: event?.type ?? 'normal' })
    })

    // ── confirmMove ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving]  →  normal end of a turn, hands off to next player (Week 3)
    // @TO: #Waiting     →  player waits for their next turn
    this.on('confirmMove', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // Week 3 will add turn rotation here.
      // For now: just let CAP apply @TO: #Waiting
    })

    // ── blockPlayer ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving]  →  called when player lands on a double-headed snake
    // @TO: #Blocked     →  player skips their next turn
    this.on('blockPlayer', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // Business logic: the position was already updated in rollDice.
      // This action just transitions state — CAP handles the @TO: #Blocked update.
      req.info('Player is blocked and will skip their next turn.')
    })

    // ── unblockPlayer ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Blocked]   →  called at the start of a blocked player's next turn
    // @TO: $flow.previous →  CAP restores the status to whatever it was before #Blocked
    this.on('unblockPlayer', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // CAP's $flow.previous does the work — no code needed here.
      // The player's status returns to #Waiting automatically.
    })
    
    //---skipturn------------------------------------
    this.on('skipTurn', 'Players', async (req) => {
      const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
      await UPDATE(Players)
      .set({ turnsBlocked: (player.turnsBlocked || 0) + 1 })
      .where({ ID: player.ID })
    })

    // ── winGame ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving]  →  called when player.position === 100
    // @TO: #Finished    →  player has won
    //this.on('winGame', 'Players', async (req) => {
      //const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
      //if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

      // Mark the session as finished with this player as winner
      //await UPDATE('snakeladder.GameSessions')
        //.set({
          //finishedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
          //winner_ID: player.ID
        //})
        //.where({ ID: player.session_ID })

      //await this.emit('GameWon', { playerID: player.ID, sessionID: player.session_ID })
    //})

    // ── winGame ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// @from: [#Moving]  →  called when player.position === 100
// @TO: #Finished    →  player has won
this.on('winGame', 'Players', async (req) => {
  const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID });

  if (!player) {
    return req.error(404, 'Player not found');
  }

  // Ensure the player has reached the last square
  if (player.position !== 100) {
    return req.error(409, 'Player has not reached square 100');
  }

  // Mark the game session as finished
  await UPDATE('snakeladder.GameSessions')
    .set({
      finishedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
      winner_ID: player.ID
    })
    .where({ ID: player.session_ID });

  // Emit the GameWon event
  await this.emit('GameWon', {
    playerID: player.ID,
    sessionID: player.session_ID
  });

  return {
    message: 'Congratulations! You won the game.'
  };
});

    return super.init()
  }
}

 

2. Response 409 for rolldice from wrong state

3. Position after DoubleSnake

4. wingame

currently, the status is directly turned to finished if wingame is called without checking players position. But, I have added the validation to even check whether current position is 100 or not to reach finished state.

entry.title: Re: Can we send the current user email address in a SAP F...
entry.content:
Good day @S0008017772 

 

Thank you for your question

In Public Cloud, the settings for Web Services, allows you to define the OCI catalog URL and it’s call structure. The call structure can pass fixed values or supported SAP fields to the catalog. SY-UNAME is the standard field for passing the current username,

For example:

Parameter name: USERNAME

Parameter value: SY-UNAME

Parameter type: SAP Field

This passes the user ID not the user’s email address.

See the following references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/spmm/5825852732.html?locale=en-US

I am not aware of any call structure that resolves the current user’s business email address. The available field selection is fixed by the delivered OCI integration. You cannot enter an arbitrary CDS field or Business Partner email field and expect to resolve it dynamically.

See the following references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/41dc8457e0d7b576e10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US

I am not aware of key user functionality that enriches the outbound OCI catalog call with additional user specific parameters such as email address. Therefore, an on-premises enhancement or unreleased BAdI would not be available in this Public Cloud environment.

Here are practical options:

You can check whether Amazon Business can identify the user using the user ID. Ensure you configure the required Amazon parameter using SY-UNAME, then maintain a corresponding user mapping in Amazon Business.

You can use a common fixed email or account value, only if Amazon accepts a shared purchasing identity. Ensure you maintain the amazon parameter as a fixed value rather than a field.

If Amazon strictly requires the individual email address, you can raise an SAP incident or feature request under the OCI purchasing application component, ensure you include confirmation from Amazon that the email should be supplied during the punch out request and you cannot derive it from the supplied user ID.

For testing, Open Settings for Web Services, select the Amazon web service, and review Call Structure.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Issue : Old Fiscal Year open for Asset master data ch...
entry.content:
Hello,

Based on your description, it appears that you are encountering error AA669.

This error occurs specifically when the FI-GL side has been closed (via transaction OB52) before FI-AA (via transaction AJAB). The system raises this message as a safeguard, in order to prevent postings on the Asset Accounting side that could impact the General Ledger books once they are already closed.

I would like to bring to your attention that there is no practical benefit in keeping FI-AA open if postings are no longer permitted in FI-GL, as both components need to remain aligned.

To resolve the situation, please consider one of the following options:

  1. Reopen FI-GL via transaction OB52; or
  2. Close FI-AA for fiscal year 2026 via transaction AJAB.

Please review the options above and proceed with the one that best fits your business requirements.

Foreword

When we launched the SAP Change Makers Podcast in the summer of 2021, we had a simple idea: give a microphone to the people who actually drive change — the leaders, experts, and practitioners inside and around SAP who turn big ideas into real outcomes for our customers.

Since then, we've explored quantum computing before it was on anyone's roadmap. We've gone behind the scenes of the SAP Mission Control Center — "the best kept secret in the industry." We've talked resilience with the teams that keep Black Friday running, and most recently, the rise of Agentic AI that is redefining what customer support even means.

What connects all these conversations? Every guest is a change maker: someone who plants an idea, drives a vision forward, and executes. And every episode gives you something you can use — in 10 to 30 minutes, on your commute, at your desk, or wherever you listen.

This article is the permanent home of the complete episode collection. Every time a new episode goes live, it appears right at the top of the list below. So bookmark this page, subscribe on your favorite platform, and check back — the newest conversation is always just one scroll away.

entry.title: Re: Standard asset report- J1IDEPCALPOST and AR02
entry.content:
In SAP, Investment Support is typically treated as a reduction of the asset's acquisition cost rather than a standard acquisition.
When maintained on the liability side, it is captured as a negative value in the depreciation area configured for this purpose.
- Check the Depreciation Area setup using T-code AO21.
- Ensure the "Negative values allowed" indicator is checked for your Investment Support depreciation area.
- If you need to shift the presentation to appear as an asset acquisition in your custom report/layout, you must adjust the display logic via T-code OAYZ or the Report Painter used for J1IDISPBLK and J1IDEPCALPOST
Best Regards
Leonardo

entry.title: Re: Asset Master Data Correction
entry.content:
If an asset already has acquisition postings but the capitalization year (ANLA-ZUJHR) is blank, SAP blocks direct editing in AS02. The system expects the capitalization date to automatically derive from the asset value date of the very first acquisition posting. When AS02 is grayed out, follow these steps to correct it:
- Check the Asset Value Date: Review the posted documents for this asset.
- The date of the first acquisition document acts as the master record's capitalization date.
- Adjust Screen Layout Rules: If the field status for the capitalization date was set to "Display" or "Hide" when the asset was originally created, it remains restricted.
- Use transaction code ORFA (or navigate via SPRO: Financial Accounting > Asset Management > Master Data > Screen Layout > Define Screen Layout for Asset Master Data) to change the status of the Capitalization Date field to Optional or Required.
Best Regards
Leonardo

entry.title: Re: Simplify the downtime-optimized System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA with Uptime Customizing for FI
entry.content:
Hi @Gabe_Mensching  !

Downtime-optimized Conversion can be combined with DMOVE2S4 approach (see Blog DMOVE2S4 – your one-step approach into the cloud). DMOVE2S4 allows for a combined (downtime-optimized) system conversion to SAP S/4HANA and move to RISE.

Downtime-optimized Conversion cannot be combined with DMO with System Move.

See also SAP Note 3682998 - Executing and Monitoring Downtime-Optimized Conversion to SAP S/4HANA with SUM 2.0 SP 26.

Best regards,

Mirja

entry.title: Re: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 1 — Model the Board
entry.content:
1. db/schema.cds

namespace snakeladder;

//Status
type TurnStatus : String enum {
    Waiting; // Session created, not yet started
    Playing; // It's this player's turn — roll the dice!
    Moving; // Dice rolled, player token is being moved
    Blocked; // Player hit a double-headed snake — skips next turn
    Finished; // This player has reached square 100 and won
};

// ── Board square — pre-loaded with snake/ladder data
entity BoardSquares {
    key square       : Integer; // 1–100
        snakeTo      : Integer; // 0 if no snake on this square
        ladderTo     : Integer; // 0 if no ladder on this square
        isDoubleHead : Boolean default false; // true if this is a double-headed snake mouth
}

// ── Game session
entity GameSessions {
    key ID         : UUID;
        name       : String(100);
        createdAt  : Timestamp;
        startedAt  : Timestamp;
        finishedAt : Timestamp;
        winner     : Association to Players;
}

// Player  — one row per player per session
@flow.status: TurnStatus
entity Players {
    key ID         : UUID;
        session    : Association to GameSessions;
        name       : String(100);

        @readonly
        position   : Integer default 1; // current square (0-100)

        @readonly
        TurnStatus : TurnStatus default #Waiting;
        turnOrder  : Integer; // 1 = goes first
        lastRoll   : Integer; // last dice result
}

2. srv/game-service.cds

using {snakeladder} from '../db/schema';
@path: 'GameService' service GameService {
entity GameSessions as projection on snakeladder.GameSessions;

        @flow.status: TurnStatus
entity Players      as projection on snakeladder.Players
    actions {

        @from: [ #Waiting]  @TO: #Playing
        action startTurn();

        @from: [ #Playing]  @TO: #Moving
        action rollDice();

        @from: [ #Moving]   @TO: #Waiting // transition to next player
        action confirmMove();

        @from: [ #Moving]   @TO: #Blocked // double-header snake bite
        action blockPlayer();

        @from: [ #Blocked]  @TO: $flow.previous // restores pre-block status
        action unblockPlayer();

        @from: [ #Moving]   @TO: #Finished
        action winGame();
    };

entity BoardSquares as projection on snakeladder.BoardSquares;
}

3. Screenshot of http://localhost:4004

4. Kept the suggested TurnStatus enum values as of now, maybe will add new ones if required during the game development / implementation.

entry.title: Successor of Run Balance Interest Calculation (T-Code F.52) for G/L in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
entry.content:
We are currently implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and require clarification regarding Balance Interest Calculation for G/L Accounts.

In SAP S/4HANA Private Edition / On-Premise, transaction F.52 (Balance Interest Calculation) was available to calculate interest on G/L account balances, which was commonly used for calculating Running Finance / Running Markup on financing accounts.

We would like to ask the following:

  1. What is the standard alternative to Transaction F.52 in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
  2. Has this functionality been replaced by another Fiori application, background job, or standard process?
  3. If there is no direct replacement, what is the SAP-recommended approach for calculating Running Finance (interest/markup on G/L balances) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
  4. Does SAP recommend using any standard functionality from Treasury, Bank Account Interest Management, or another component for this business requirement?
  5. If this functionality is not available in the standard Public Cloud solution, is there any recommended extensibility approach or best practice that SAP suggests?

Our business requirement is to calculate periodic financing charges (running finance/markup) based on the outstanding balance of a G/L account.

We would appreciate your guidance on the recommended standard solution and SAP best practices for this requirement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Thank you for your support.

Kind regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Successor of Run Balance Interest Calculation (T-Code...
entry.content:
Please update that the G/L Account Interest Scale job in the Schedule General Ledger Jobs app, and the Run Balance Interest Calculation app are deprecated for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition from release 2105.

SAP recommends you to not keep loans as pure G/L accounts.

Because handling loans with pure G/L postings might not fulfill today's accounting standards. Manual workarounds might be needed in the future.

That's why there is no successor app available that would replace the old app G/L Account Interest Scale.

If you don´t use any specific application like SAP Treasury and Risk Management, the recommendation is to switch to AP/AR-based loan handling, to enable follow-up processes like discounting of long-term receivables and impairment postings.

For interest accounting you can use the Schedule Interest Calculation Jobs application.

See also:

  • 3084743 - Incorrect and missing information in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2108 "G/L Account Interest Scale / Run Balance Interest Calculation" What´s New
  • 3030116 - Deprecation of G/L account Interest scale
  • Schedule Interest Calculation Jobs
  • Display Interest Runs

entry.title: Re: Incorrect VAT reporting date transferred from SD to F...
entry.content:
Good day @KallaiG 

Thank you for your question

 

Thank you for the detailed examples. You cannot treat these three scenarios the same way.

Scenario 1: Cancellation invoice:

The result you got is consistent with standard reserve behaviour when an invoice is cancelled, the Tax Reporting Date is copied from the original accounting document. SAP provides the released custom logic Reversal: Tax Reporting Date Derivation for changing the behaviour and refers to SAP for additional information.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/052b9c8012254d7c9c98462524b44b5c.html?locale=en-US

Therefore, 30 June on the cancellation is not necessary a defect. You should evaluate the reversal BAdI to derive 7 July because this is required for the Hungarian company code.

 

Scenario 2: Collective invoice

Sales billing determines the Tax Fulfillment Date and Tax Reporting Date automatically according to the applicable business rules, and you can verify both values in the resulting journal entry. However, i am not aware of any official documentation stating that one date should come from the first billing item while the other comes from the last item. If the configured rule requires the latest fulfillment date, inconsistent item selection should be reported for analysis rather than corrected through an unsupported enhancement.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a624a4a6d1d8473eb95fb42660127cfe/c5ed70d722fd434eb4da2a9aca4a1434.html?locale=en-US

 

Scenario 3: Final invoice with down payment

You can only apply OPERIO, when the transaction is classified by the corresponding periodic business transaction. If an ordinary final invoice with down payment clearing is unexpectedly classified as OPERIO, and the maintained derivation rule is consequently ignored, this requires support analysis. I am not aware of any configuration setting that safely overrides the internal business transaction classification.

 

For scenario 2 and 3, you can reproduce each case separately and attach:

  1. The billing document and accounting document numbers
  2. The configured derivation rule and business transaction
  3. All item fulfillment dates and the billing date
  4. The result two tax dates
  5. The cancellation or down payment document flow
  6. Verify both tax date field from Manage Journal Entries

Ensure you confirm the expected Hungarian VAT treatment with the customer’s Hungarian tax adviser. Tax fulfillment Date is defined as the point when tax becomes chargeable and the Tax Reporting Date as the reporting period date, with the reporting date not earlier than the fulfillment date.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/60a09f68f2444ceca31dcac2e7017945/521fa8d1586f4cbc9e0d9013d0804b94.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: How to receive & pick full and partial boxes in s...
entry.content:
Good day @srinivasareddybhimanadham 

Thank you for your question

Yes, there is an available solution in Public Cloud Warehouse management. The correct design depends on whether your business only needs to process quantities in cartons and pieces or the business must track each physical carton separately.

You can maintain the product with:

  • Base unit of measure, for example PC
  • Alternative unit of measure, for example CAR
  • Conversion, for example 1 CAR = 20 PC

Receiving, warehouse processing, stock transfer, and picking in units that differ from the product base unit. For warehouse execution, you can use the Stock Specific Unit of Measure, or SUoM function. SUoM represents the physical packaging of stock, such as cartons, throughout warehouse processes. A carton is used as an alternative unit containing a defined number of pieces.

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/a07cbd534f22b44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/a59ae8b2030645678bef278d40d2dade.html?

Here are areas you should check:

  • In Manage Product Master Data, you should maintain PC as the base unit and CAR as an alternative unit with the correct conversion.                                                                                                                
  • In the Warehouse product data for the warehouse number, ensure you maintain the preferred unit of measure as CAR where carton processing is required.
  • You can receive full cartons using CAR. During picking, you should enter either 1 CAR for a full carton or the required quantity in PC for a partial carton.

In RF processing, the system displays the preferred unit where it is maintained, but the warehouse worker can change the quantity and unit of measure during confirmation. The outbound delivery can also transfer an alternative unit to warehouse Management when that unit is used as the SUom.

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/d941e4fbb9954ed1a96733dd7d3347a3.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/bf0a56972ffc4a4fb4cfbb10f9c36484.html?locale=en-US

You can test this process using Process Warehouse Tasks – Picking. Open the warehouse task and ensure you confirm one test first in the CAR and then another test in PC.

A limitation is necessary because SUoM manages quantities and packaging units, but it does not uniquely identify each physical carton.  If your business must track individual cartons, carton numbers, labels, or the remaining contents of each opened carton, you can use the Handling Units. Handling Unit Management tracks the packaging structure and the materials contained in each physical package.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/b4c3ead97179498b900e7a3e808c03af.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite : GitHub integration in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Introduction 

SAP Cloud Integration version 6.80/(8.x --> 8.44 ), one of the capabilities of SAP Integration Suite, comes with a feature which will allow you to integrate the GitHub to the integration suite to perform the basic GitHub operations on the design time integration artifacts. To know more, you can refer the help documentation.

In this blog post, you will understand the detailed aspects of this feature, Why, What, its objectives, principles and how to leverage in the Cloud integration capability of the integration suite.

Why GitHub integration is essential?

  • No GitHub integration in the Integration Suite for collaborative development, track changes, storage, backup, or recovery.
  • Users must manually download and upload artifacts in GitHub, causing media breakage.
  • Manual process is slow, error-prone, and impacts version control, reducing productivity for effective collaborative developments.

What is GitHub Integration?

  • Integrates Integration Suite with GitHub for secure repository connection, Git authentication, and operations directly in-app.
  • Streamlines artifact management, reduces errors, enhances version control, and supports collaborative developments and CI/CD compatibility.

Benefits/Value proposition

  • Collaboration: Streamlined teamwork via branches and pull requests
  • Seamless Integration : No media breakage between Integration suite and GitHub
  • Governance : Github repositories maintained in Integration suite via Tenant administrator
  • DevOps: Enables CI/CD automation
  • Backup: Secure storage for artifacts with easy recovery.
  • Distributed: Work locally and sync changes.
  • Rollback: Revert to previous versions quickly.
  • Traceability: Track changes for auditing.
  • Scalability: Supports large and distributed teams.
  • Security: Protect artifacts with permissions and authentication.

Availability

GitHub integration in the integration suite for cloud integration capability is available in the trial, standard and above/higher editions.

How to leverage?

Let me explain the workflow and steps into two parts.

  • How to Successfully Configure GitHub Repositories for Cloud Integration?
  • How to successfully import/clone, push, Pull the integration artifacts from/to Github?

How to Successfully Configure GitHub Repositories for Cloud Integration?

To integrate GitHub repositories successfully, follow these easy steps:

Prerequisites:

  1. Ensure your account has a Tenant Admin role (refer to Cloud Integration documentation if unsure).
  2. Generate a Personal Access Token (PAT) with the required permissions for the repositories.

How to Create a Personal Access Token (PAT):

  1. Log in to GitHub, go to Settings -- >  Developer Settings -- > Personal Access Tokens.
  2. Choose Fine-Grained or Classic Tokens and click Generate New Token. Set an expiry for the token.

Simple Setup Steps:

  1. In the Cloud Integration portal, navigate to Settings -- > Integrations -- > Git Access.
  2. Click Edit in the Repository Details table and select Add to set up a new repository.
  3. Enter your PAT and GitHub repository URL.
  4. Click Connect to link the repository (Name and Description will auto-fill).
  5. Click Add to save the repository details in the table.

Notes:

  • You can integrate up to 50 repositories.
  • Make sure to click Save after completing all configurations.

Follow these steps to configure your GitHub repositories for seamless Cloud Integration!

View Github Repostories

Add Github Repository

Edit Github Repository 

How to successfully import/clone, push, Pull the integration artifacts from/to Github?

  • Git Import use case & experience 
  • Git Push use case & experience
  • Git Pull use case and experience

Git Import use case & experience 

Prerequisites:

  • Ensure Git repositories are configured by the tenant admin.
  • Have a Personal Access Token (PAT) with permission for the repositories.

Steps for Importing Integration Flows:

  1. In the Design section, go to Integration Packages.
  2. Select an integration package and click Edit.
  3. Open the Artifacts tab and select Add Integration Flow.
  4. Choose Add Integrations Manually (requires Premium plan).
  5. Use Git Import to import the flow:
    • Enter your PAT.
    • Select a repository and branch.
    • Choose the desired flow and click Add or Add and Open in Editor.

Outcome:
The flow is imported into the integration package, enabling Git operations like push and pull for collaboration and version control

Add Integration flow from Github repository, branch

Git Push use case & experience

Git Push lets you upload local changes from an integration artifact to a connected remote Git repository, ensuring your repository is fully updated for collaboration and version control.

Prerequisites Before Git Push:

  • Configured Repository: Your admin should set up the necessary repositories (see Git Access guide for details).

  • Access Permissions: Have the right permissions and a Personal Access Token (PAT) ready.

Steps to Push Changes:

  1. Navigate to Integration Packages: Go to Design > Integrations and APIs to find the list of integration packages.

  2. Select the Package: Choose the package with the artifact you want to push and click Artifacts.

  3. Start Git Push: Click the ... (triple dot) icon on the artifact and select Git Push.

  4. Fill in the Details:

    • Add your PAT for authentication.

    • Choose the repository name and branch.

  5. Commit Message: Enter a clear message describing your changes.

  6. Push Changes: Click Push to upload updates to the remote repository.

Key Notes:

  • The repository will be updated with the latest version of your artifact.

  • Ensure branch and repository details are correct to avoid errors or conflicts.

Git Push operation to push the local changes to the Github repository, branch

Git Pull use case and experience

Git Pull updates your local files by fetching and merging the latest changes from a remote Git repository, keeping everything in sync.

Before You Start

  1. Ensure repositories are set up by the administrator (Check Git Access documentation).
  2. Git Pull works only for imported artifacts.
Steps to Perform Git Pull
  1. Open Integration Packages:
    Go to Design -- > Integrations and APIs to find integration packages.
  2. Select a Package:
    Click on the package and then choose Artifacts to view its contents.
  3. Use Git Pull:
    Find the artifact to update and click the Git Pull icon.
  4. Verify Details:
    Confirm the displayed artifact info and enter your Personal Access Token (PAT).
  5. Finish Up:
    Click Pull to update your local artifact with the latest changes.

This helps you easily keep your files updated for smooth collaboration.

Git Pull operation to pull the latest changes from the Github repository, branch

FAQs

  1. Supported Integration Artifacts: Basic Git operations are supported for Integration Flows in the initial release.
  2. Available Editions: GitHub integration is available in the Trial, Standard, and higher editions of the Integration Suite.
  3. Repository Limit: You can add up to 50 GitHub repositories in the integration settings.
  4. Impact of Removing Repositories: Removing repositories from the integration settings has no effect on integration flows—they remain in the design workspace and GitHub, if previously pushed. However, the integration flow will be de-linked from GitHub.
  5. Supported Platforms: Only GitHub on the cloud is supported on-premise GitHub is not.
  6. Supported Cloud Platform : GitLab.
  7. Authentication: GitHub operations are supported through PAT tokens only.
  8. PAT Token Persistence: PAT tokens are not store, you must manually provide one for each GitHub operation.
  9. Repository Descriptions: Descriptions for GitHub repositories can be set during creation on GitHub and will be visible when imported into the Integration Suite.
  10. Differentiate local and remote changes: Github repository name, branch name is tagged to the integration flow available in the remote branch. Integration flow is tagged in the design workspace and monitoring view(for deployed artifact).

What’s coming next?

  • Introduce CRUD functionality for user Personal Access Tokens (PAT).
  • Add support for more authentication types and cloud platforms like GitLab.
  • Extend compatibility with on-premise GitHub repositories.
  • Enhance Git operations like import, push, pull for the remaining integration artifacts.
  • Implement conflict resolution tools (graphical/text editor) for integration artifacts and resources.
  • Support multi-branch management for integration artifacts.
  • Enable public OData Remote APIs for CI/CD processes.
  • Expand Git support for integration packages.

Other source of material

  • https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/isuite-integrations-and-apis/git-access
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/isuite-integrations-and-apis/git-push
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/isuite-integrations-and-apis/git-pull
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/isuite-integrations-and-apis/import-integration-flows-from-git-repository

I hope you find the feature in this blog post beneficial. If you have any questions or feedback, please leave a comment.

entry.title: Feedback on SAP Certification AI Scoring Transparency and Exam Reliability
entry.content:
I would like to share some constructive feedback regarding the SAP certification experience, particularly around the AI-based scoring process and the reliability of certification exams. I have attended the C_THR83 Performance and Goals Management where I had a very unreliable scoring and still awaiting response from SAP Learning Support Team!

While I appreciate SAP's continued investment in modernizing its certification program, I believe there is room for improvement in the transparency of the AI-based scoring system. At present, candidates receive only an overall score, with no visibility into how individual tasks or responses were evaluated. Without detailed feedback, it is difficult to understand where improvements are needed or to have confidence in the assessment outcome.

It would be beneficial if SAP could consider:

  • Providing a breakdown of scores by task or competency area.
  • Offering more detailed feedback on evaluated responses.
  • Introducing a formal score review or re-evaluation process for candidates who believe their results may warrant further review.

In addition to scoring transparency, many candidates have reported recurring technical issues during certification exams. Although SAP regularly performs maintenance on the certification platform, some issues continue to occur, including:

  • Portlets not displaying correctly.
  • Workflows not triggering despite Rule Trace confirming the business rules executed successfully.
  • Difficulties starting exams.
  • Other intermittent platform stability issues that can affect the overall exam experience.

Another area that could benefit from improvement is the responsiveness of support. When candidates encounter technical issues or have concerns about exam results, timely communication and clear updates from the support team are essential to maintaining confidence in the certification process.

SAP certifications represent a significant investment of time, effort, and resources for candidates. For that reason, transparency, technical reliability, and an effective support process are all important in ensuring that the certification experience is both fair and trusted.

I hope SAP will continue to enhance the certification program by:

  • Increasing transparency in AI-based scoring.
  • Providing candidates with more meaningful assessment feedback.
  • Introducing a structured review or appeal process for scoring.
  • Continuing to improve the stability and reliability of certification exams.
  • Strengthening support responsiveness and communication when candidates report issues.

These improvements would help reinforce confidence in SAP certifications and provide candidates with a clearer understanding of their performance while ensuring a more reliable and positive certification experience.

entry.title: SAP Incentive Management: Leveraging BusinessObjects APIs for Automated Reporting
entry.content:
Introduction

Incentive compensation plays a critical role in motivating sales teams and driving business performance. Organizations using SAP Incentive Management (formerly CallidusCloud Commissions) often rely on reports and analytics to monitor commissions, validate calculations, track performance, and provide timely insights to stakeholders.

While SAP Incentive Management offers powerful reporting capabilities through SAP BusinessObjects (BO), many organizations still depend on manual report generation and distribution processes. As reporting requirements grow, manual execution becomes time-consuming, error-prone, and difficult to scale.

This blog explores how SAP BusinessObjects APIs can be used to automate report scheduling, extraction, and distribution, enabling organizations to create efficient and scalable reporting solutions for SAP Incentive Management.

The Reporting Challenge

Typical incentive management reporting requirements include:

  • Commission statements for payees
  • Credit and payment summaries
  • Sales performance dashboards
  • Compensation plan effectiveness reports
  • Audit and compliance reports
  • Monthly and quarterly executive reporting

Organizations frequently encounter challenges such as:

  • Manual report execution
  • Repetitive report distribution activities
  • Delays in report availability
  • Dependency on administrators
  • Difficulty integrating reports with external systems

Automation addresses these challenges by allowing reports to be generated and delivered without human intervention.

Why Use BusinessObjects APIs?

SAP Incentive Management reporting is commonly built on the SAP BusinessObjects platform. BusinessObjects provides a comprehensive set of APIs through its RESTful Web Services that enable developers to programmatically interact with reports and report schedules.

Using BO APIs, organizations can:

  • Schedule reports automatically
  • Execute reports on demand
  • Retrieve report outputs in multiple formats
  • Monitor report execution status
  • Distribute reports through email or external applications
  • Integrate reporting with enterprise workflows

 List of APIs

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/is-webi-supported
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/period-report-folders
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/ods-reports-config
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/report-template-names
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/report-template-names-by-type-and-recipient
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/report-shortcuts
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/period-report-folders
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/is-webi-supported
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

 

API Documentation
https://<tenant-id>.app.commissions.cloud.sap/taas/swagger-ui/index.html#/ 

Bonus Tip

All the tables related to BO are in the TCMP Schema with reporting table starting with TAAS_%

entry.title: Re: How to set up free of charge purchase item in public...
entry.content:
Good day @Ian2345 

Thank you for your question

For a material that must always be purchased free of charge, a purchasing info record with price 0 is not the preferred solution. Condition type PPR0 represents a purchasing price, and the system validates that its rate is greater than zero. A free of charge PO item is instead controlled by the purchasing item indicators, particularly whether an invoice is expected.

The BAdI Change of Purchase Requisition Item (MM_PUR_S4_PR_MODIFY_ITEM) is released for changing selected standard and extension fields in purchase requisition. InvoiceisExpected is part of the purcahase requisition item interface.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/720dcec59d264114bc4ec19759aa4514.html?locale=en-US

Your code should use a Boolean value:

IF purchaserequisitionitem-material = `XXX`

Purchaserequisitionitemchange-invoiceisexpected = abap false.

ENDIF.

However, this only controls the Invoice Receipt expected indicator in the purchase requisition. It does not provide a material master settling that permanently classifies every future purchase order for that material as free of charge. Depending on how your PO is created, the PO item may still require the purchaser to select Free of Charge manually.

Here is a preferred solution:

You can use the PR BAdI to default InvoiceIsExpected = abap false, then enforce the requirement at purchase order level with the released BAdI Final Check before Saving Purchase Order (BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO). BAdI for custom checks on selected standard purchase order fields are recorded before saving.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/044700714fc54863b7fa9ea0b5654ba0.html?locale=en-US

Your check should raise an error when the material equals XXX or your PO item is not marked free of charge, then invoice receipt is still expected. This prevents your users or automatic PO creation processes from saving an incorrectly price item. The PO check BAdI validates the document but does not automatically change the standard free of charge field.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/8676e556cca85267e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

Here is a quick Test:

  1. You can publish the PR BAdl implementation in Custom Logic.
  2. Ensure you create a new PR for material XXX
  3. Verify that Invoice Receipt is not expected.
  4. Ensure you convert the PR into PO
  5. Open the item in Manage Purchase Orders or Create Purchase Order – Advanced.
  6. Ensure you confirm that Free of Charge is selected, and no invoice receipt is expected.
  7. Attempt to save after removing the indicator. The final check should block saving.

 

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: ACL for Products in SAP Integration Suite Developer Hub
entry.content:
Hi @btpnerd ,

This is the way how can you configure it from scratch:

  • Login to the desired subaccount
  • Create role based on the template called 'Application Developer' -> Click on 'Create a Role'
    • Name it with the desired naming convention
    • Use 'Static' as the 'Source'
    • To fill the 'Values' filed is optional but you can fill with a description/context of the custom role
    • In the Next Page you can select a role collection this is also optional if it is not required you can simply click on 'Next' and 'Finish' the custom role creation
  • Go to 'Role Collection' and click 'Create' and name it with the desired naming convention
  • Now go to the recently created Role Collection and add the newly created Custom Role by clicking the 'Edit' button and than save this (You can have multiple roles assigned to a single role collection)
  • As a last step you can assign to the desired users this custom role

Now you can go to the API Portal configure that end of the scenario:

  • Go to the selected API Product
  • Go to the Permission tab
  • Use the previously created custom role from the drop-down list for the Discovery and/or Subscription to limit the access of this API Product
  • Once you configured and saved it you can go to the Developer Hub to verify this setting

Please let me know if you have any further question about it.

Thank you,
David

entry.title: Re: How to set up free of charge purchase item in public...
entry.content:
Dear @Ian2345,

A purchasing info record with a price of 0 is not the correct solution. Public Cloud a purchase order item for which an invoice is expected cannot have a net price of 0. This explains the error raised for condition type PPR0. In the Manage Purchase Orders app a free-of-charge item is represented by deselecting Invoice Receipt under Process Flow. Once the indicator is deselected the system sets the item price to 0

The attempted BAdI implementation at purchase requisition level is not a viable mechanism for this requirement. As observed, the indicator is not set in the purchase requisition, and even if it were, this would not guarantee a free-of-charge item in the resulting purchase order. The requirement must therefore be enforced at purchase order item level

Automatic defaulting of the Invoice Receipt indicator is not documented as a supported change in the released MM_PUR_S4_PO_MODIFY_ITEM BAdI. Although InvoiceIsExpected is available in the BAdI data model, this does not mean that it is available as a changing field. SAP documents only a defined selection of standard fields that can be modified using this BAdI, and InvoiceIsExpected is not included in that list

The recommended supported solution is therefore:

  1. The purchaser deselects Invoice Receipt for material XXX in the purchase order item. The system then sets the item price to 0
  2. Implement the BAdI Final Check Before Saving Purchase Order (BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO) to prevent the purchase order from being saved when material XXX still has:
    • InvoiceIsExpected = X, or
    • a net price different from 0.

This validation ensures that the material cannot accidentally be ordered as a chargeable item. SAP documents BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO as the released BAdI for implementing customer-specific checks and error messages before a purchase order is saved

The field-control BAdI MM_PUR_S4_PO_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE is intended only to change the UI properties of supported purchase order item fields, for example to hidden, read-only, mandatory, or optional. It does not assign or change business values. Therefore, it cannot be used to automatically deselect Invoice Receipt and does not replace the final validation implemented with BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO

If completely automatic creation without any manual purchaser action is mandatory, a separate integration-based process would be required to create the purchase order with InvoiceIsExpected = false through a released purchase order interface. Automatic material dependent defaulting of this indicator in the standard Manage Purchase Orders UI is not available through the documented key-user extensibility

More details below:

SAP KBA 3069390 – How to Set a Purchase Order Item as Free of Charges in Manage Purchase Order App

SAP Help – Manage Purchase Orders

SAP Help – App Extensibility: Manage Purchase Orders

SAP KBA 3268822 – Field Control in Purchase Orders and Purchase Requisitions in S/4HANA Cloud

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Spot Tendering in SAP TM -No contract rate for carrier and base charge mandatory for carrier ranking
entry.content:
Overview

Organizations frequently face transportation procurement challenges when a carrier agreement exists, but contract rates are unavailable for specific transportation lanes. In traditional processes, mandatory freight charges defined in calculation sheets can cause charge calculation errors, preventing efficient carrier selection and delaying shipment execution.

SAP S/4HANA Advanced TM provides an effective way to address this challenge through Spot Tendering. By leveraging spot tendering capabilities, organizations can continue procuring transportation services from approved carriers while maintaining operational continuity and supply chain resilience.

After reading this blog, readers will understand the challenges and solutions in spot tendering when contract rates are missing for carrier having freight agreement and subcontracting based on contract rates existing for carriers.

Business Scenario and Challenge:
Consider the spot tender in business scenario where

  1. Certain charges like base freight charge are mandatory for carrier ranking during subcontracting on freight order in SAP TM.
  2. Carrier Freight agreement exists, contract rates are missing on certain lane.
  3. Finance team wants to still have the visibility and audit of charges breakup on freight order as per the agreement with carrier like fuel surcharge, accessorial charges.
  4. But since the base freight charge is mandatory and is missing on the lane, system gives a calculation error on freight order once the spot tender from carrier is awarded.

Solution: A practical and standard solution is to create a dedicated tendering calculation sheet that retains mandatory charge validation during carrier ranking but relaxes mandatory charge requirements after tender execution. An additional calculation sheet can be assigned to freight agreement line items with preconditions. This approach minimizes implementation effort and avoids custom development.

Through this setup we ensure the mandatory charges check to be performed only during the carrier subcontracting (Carrier ranking). And once the tender process is executed, then during charge calculation system selects a calculation sheet which does not have mandatory charge.

  1. Maintain an additional charge calculation sheet line on freight agreements for all required carriers.

Take as an example, create new calculation sheet say ‘TCCS_FTL_TEND_*, as a copy of the existing calculation sheet TCCS_FTL_*. We maintain the required charge lines as not mandatory charges in TCCS_FTL_TEND_* where as the same charge lines are mandatory in TCCS_FTL_*

Sample tendering result from SAP S/4 Test system.

Spot tender sent to carrier, accepted by carrier and awarded to carrier

Charges are calculated upon tender being awarded to carrier.

Conclusion

By combining SAP S/4HANA Advanced TM spot tendering with charge calculation rules and strategies, we can overcome the challenges in carrier ranking during subcontracting, charge calculation in case of spot tendering and provide compliance, efficiency, and financial reporting.

Benefits:
• Improved carrier participation in spot bidding while retaining requirements for contracted carriers
• Finance would have visibility of charges breakup
• Lower operational effort through automation.
